changes.detail: handleSendRequirement now posts directly to /api/agent/session and aborts after 45 seconds instead of waiting on Socket.IO agent:session:start acknowledgement.
socketio.run now uses threaded=True to reduce long-polling saturation under concurrent UI polling.

next_step: Hard refresh the browser with Ctrl+Shift+R and retry the AgentStudio Codex start button. If it fails now, the UI should leave Abriendo Codex after the HTTP error or 45 second timeout instead of staying stuck.
residual_risks: Existing browser tabs may still show old Socket.IO 400 Invalid session entries until they are hard-refreshed.
The project folder sesion-20260524210420 was created during the user earlier click and was intentionally left untouched.
CyberLACE remains globally in monitor mode for UI stability; enforce credential testing remains available through scripts/test_cyberlace_live_credentials.sh.
root_cause: Browser kept stale Socket.IO polling sessions after server restarts.
Backend log showed POST /socket.io 400 Invalid session while the UI button was waiting for the socket acknowledgement.
No backend Codex session was created during the stuck state; /api/agent/sessions returned an empty list.
summary: Fixed the AgentStudio Codex start button stuck on Abriendo Codex by removing its dependency on Socket.IO ack and starting sessions through the REST endpoint with a timeout.

validation.result: No agent:session:start dependency remains in AgentStudio source; agent_session_start_timeout is present.

validation.expected: Endpoint responds quickly with missing_requirement instead of hanging.
